pr.title: Match ef_061D 100% and rename to efspecial (special effects)
pr.author: hourianto

summary: Unmerged PR attempting to finish-match the special-effects dispatcher at 0x80061D70 and rename ef_80061D70/ef_061D toward efSync_SpawnSpecial/efspecial. The diff rewrote the large gfx_id switch around direct va_arg use, named several local data/sdata2 constants, updated symbols and callsites, and used assert/include macro tricks around jobj.h to match. Reviewers flagged the macro/inline approach and merge-conflict/rename handling, then closed the PR because the function had already been matched upstream and this version did not improve code quality; rename-only work was suggested as a separate PR.
what_changed: Renamed the symbol at .text:0x80061D70 from ef_80061D70 to efSync_SpawnSpecial in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Named local string/data symbols used by the function, including ef_803BF9D0, ef_804D39D8, ef_804D39E0, and sdata2 constants ef_804D81D0 through ef_804D81F0.

Reworked src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c around void* efSync_SpawnSpecial(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* arg_gobj, void* vlist), replacing the prior ef_80061D70 signature and EF061D_VA_ARG helper with direct va_arg calls.
Added baselib and Dolphin includes plus ft/inlines.h, introduced HSD_ASSERT macro overrides before including baselib/jobj.h, and restored HSD_ASSERT afterward.
Added explicit match-oriented stack shaping with PAD_STACK(0x98), pad arrays, and locals such as ret_obj, eff_1, eff_2, jobj_1, jobj_2, f64_1, f32_1, and cnt.
Converted some raw fighter/user_data access in the effect switch to GET_FIGHTER(arg_gobj), FighterBone parts, and joint indexing.
Added constants at the end of the function file for 0.0f, +/-M_PI_2, and 6.0f.
Changed src/melee/ef/ef_061D.h guard from MELEE_EF_EF061D70_H to MELEE_EF_EFSPECIAL_H and declared efSync_SpawnSpecial with an address comment.
Updated efSync_Spawn to include ef_061D.h and call efSync_SpawnSpecial for gfx_id < 0x4BA instead of the old ef_80061D70 extern.
Changed HSD_JObjAnimAll calls in ef_061D.c, efsync.c, and efasync.c to use ((HSD_JObj**) &efLib_80458EE0)[cnt] instead of field access through the previous struct-like view.
The PR title and review paths refer to efspecial.c, but the final changed_files slice still lists ef_061D.c/h; evidence for the file rename in the final diff is mixed.
smart_moves: Tried to give the 0x80061D70 helper a semantic name, efSync_SpawnSpecial, matching its role as the special-effect branch called from efSync_Spawn.
Moved magic float/double values such as +/-M_PI_2 and 6.0f into named sdata2 symbols to preserve data layout while making comparisons and rotations searchable.
Used existing fighter/JObj helper macros such as GET_FIGHTER and GET_JOBJ in places where the older code used raw offsets, which was a readability improvement in the attempted match.
Kept the caller relationship explicit by adding an ef_061D.h prototype and including it from efsync.c rather than relying on a local extern declaration.
The author reported using local tooling and a final game-file hash check; that is a good validation habit, although reviewers still rejected the code-quality tradeoffs.
decomp_lessons: Do not let a 100% match make the source less maintainable: this PR was closed because reviewers judged it did not improve code quality and the function had already been matched upstream.
Before landing a large match, re-check current upstream/master to avoid duplicating work that has already been solved.
Avoid macro/include hackery around shared headers when the pattern likely comes from an existing inline; reviewer feedback specifically said a jobj.h inline should not need to be redefined.
Separate mechanical file renames from matching work, or delay renames until just before merge, to reduce conflicts and make review cleaner.
If exact assert strings from inlined baselib functions are needed, prefer fixing or using the real header inline behavior over redefining HSD_ASSERT for one translation unit.
Match-oriented casts like ((HSD_JObj**) &efLib_80458EE0)[cnt] may be useful clues, but future cleanup should investigate the correct declaration/type rather than leaving opaque casts.
Changing va_list-facing prototypes to void* can be a matching tactic, but it should be documented and reconciled with callers because efSync_Spawn passes an active va_list through this helper.
naming_conventions: ef_80061D70 -> efSync_SpawnSpecial for the sync special-effect dispatcher.
Planned module/file name: efspecial for special effects, though the final slice still shows ef_061D.c/h.
Header guard target changed to MELEE_EF_EFSPECIAL_H.
Address-backed unknown data names used the module/address style: ef_803BF9D0, ef_804D39D8, ef_804D39E0, ef_804D81D0, ef_804D81D8, ef_804D81E0, ef_804D81E8, ef_804D81EC, ef_804D81F0.
Shared switch-case join label used a function-scoped descriptive form: efSync_SpawnSpecial_case_4B6_4B7.
Temporary decomp locals followed generic matching names such as eff_1, eff_2, jobj_1, jobj_2, f64_1, and f32_1; reviewers did not endorse these as final-quality names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used direct va_arg(vlist, Type*) extraction for Vec3*, HSD_JObj*, f32*, and void* arguments in the large gfx_id switch.
Added PAD_STACK(0x98) plus explicit stack pad arrays and a Vec3 local to shape the stack frame.
Promoted literal constants to exact-address sdata2 globals for float/double loads and rodata/sdata ordering.
Temporarily redefined HSD_ASSERT before including baselib/jobj.h so inlined JObj setters/getters would emit original-looking assert file/message strings; this was specifically criticized in review.
Used a goto label to share the common tail for cases 0x4B6 and 0x4B7.
Used pointer reinterpretation of efLib_80458EE0 for HSD_JObjAnimAll calls to match the generated access pattern.
Included ft/inlines.h to access helper inlines/macros such as GET_FIGHTER and GET_JOBJ.
review_feedback: PsiLupan: the redefined pattern was indicative of an inline from jobj.h and should not require redefining it.

hourianto clarified that the ugly macro hackery was at the top of the file, used instead of many defines or duplicating inline functions, and described some in-function inline matching as extremely brittle.
ribbanya review: merge conflicts needed to be resolved more cleanly, and the file rename should probably wait until just before merging.
ribbanya review comment: a TODO had been added because the issue did not seem straightforward.
ribbanya closing comment: the PR did not improve the function's code quality, the function was already matched upstream, and the file rename could be done separately.
decomp.dev bot reported only small unmatched-item improvements in efSync_Spawn and efAsync_80063930, not a decisive quality signal.
follow_up_queries: Find the upstream PR or commit that matched ef_061D/efSync_SpawnSpecial and compare its cleaner solution.
Audit baselib/jobj.h inline/assert handling so effect code does not need local HSD_ASSERT redefinitions.
Split any remaining ef_061D -> efspecial file rename into a rename-only PR after conflicts are resolved.
Investigate the correct type/declaration for efLib_80458EE0 to remove opaque HSD_JObj** casts.
Check whether efSync_SpawnSpecial should keep a va_list prototype instead of void* while still matching.
